prompt: Bars are planned hours per week against a 40h capacity. Tap every week that must be levelled first or the project slips.
guard: count([hours[0]>40 && cpFlags[0]==1, hours[1]>40 && cpFlags[1]==1, hours[2]>40 && cpFlags[2]==1, hours[3]>40 && cpFlags[3]==1, hours[4]>40 && cpFlags[4]==1, hours[5]>40 && cpFlags[5]==1]) >= 1 && count([hours[0]>40, hours[1]>40, hours[2]>40, hours[3]>40, hours[4]>40, hours[5]>40]) >= count([hours[0]>40 && cpFlags[0]==1, hours[1]>40 && cpFlags[1]==1, hours[2]>40 && cpFlags[2]==1, hours[3]>40 && cpFlags[3]==1, hours[4]>40 && cpFlags[4]==1, hours[5]>40 && cpFlags[5]==1]) + 1

derived.nCorr: count([hours[0]>40 && cpFlags[0]==1, hours[1]>40 && cpFlags[1]==1, hours[2]>40 && cpFlags[2]==1, hours[3]>40 && cpFlags[3]==1, hours[4]>40 && cpFlags[4]==1, hours[5]>40 && cpFlags[5]==1])

items.label: 'Week ' + (i+1)
items.why: (h > 40 && cpFlags[i]==1) ? ('Booked ' + h + 'h (over by ' + (h-40) + 'h) and on the critical path — level this first.') : (h > 40 ? ('Over by ' + (h-40) + 'h, but off the critical path — levelling here won’t protect the end date.') : (cpFlags[i]==1 ? ('Critical-path work, but ' + h + 'h is within the 40h capacity.') : ('Within capacity and off the critical path.')))

explanation: Level the weeks that are BOTH over capacity AND on the critical path first — easing over-allocation off the critical path doesn't protect the finish date. {{nCorr}} week(s) qualify here.

prompt: Bars are planned hours per week against a 40h capacity; 'CP' marks critical-path weeks. What should you do about the over-allocations?

choices.text: 'Level the over-allocated CRITICAL-PATH week(s) first — those over-allocations threaten the finish date.'
choices.why: mode==0 ? ('Weeks over 40h AND on the critical path (' + nOverCP + ' here) have no float, so the over-allocation pushes the finish — level those first.') : 'No over-allocated week is on the critical path here, so this is not the priority.'

choices.text: 'The over-allocations are all OFF the critical path — smooth them if capacity allows, but the finish date is not at risk.'
choices.why: mode==1 ? ('Every over-allocated week (' + nOver + ') has float, so easing them protects nobody’s deadline — the finish is safe; smoothing within float is optional.') : 'At least one over-allocated week here IS on the critical path, so the finish IS at risk.'

choices.text: 'Immediately add people to every over-allocated week.'
choices.why: 'Adding resources everywhere ignores float and cost; level within float and only protect the critical path.'

choices.text: 'Nothing — resource over-allocation never affects the schedule.'
choices.why: 'Over-allocation on the critical path directly threatens the finish date.'

explanation: Only over-allocation on the CRITICAL PATH threatens the finish (zero float). Off the critical path, the float absorbs it — smooth within the float if you can, but it isn't urgent.
